title: podman generate systemd contains wrong PIDFile path on CentOS 7
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** On a CentOS 7 machine the `podman generate systemd ` command generates a service file with a wrong `PIDFile=` path. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Run a `CentOS Linux release 7.7.1908 (Core)` (this was true for 7.6 too) release and use the packaged `podman` version from `extras`. 2. Create a container with podman. 3. Run `podman generate systemd > /etc/systemd/system/speedtest.service` and reload the daemon to pick up the new service. 4. Attempt to start / enable the container through systemd with `systemctl start speedtest`. **Describe the results you received:** Somewhat confusing error `Job for speedtest.service failed because a configured resource limit was exceeded.`. The culprit appears to be the `PIDFile`, which can't be opened.
Full logs ``` # systemctl start speedtest.service Job for speedtest.service failed because a configured resource limit was exceeded. See "systemctl status speedtest.service" and "journalctl -xe" for details. # systemctl status speedtest.service ● speedtest.service - aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105ac323b83b838b2bc67567bb073139ddf7 Podman Container Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/speedtest.service; disabled; vendor preset: disabled) Drop-In: /etc/systemd/system/speedtest.service.d └─override.conf Active: failed (Result: start-limit) since Tue 2019-09-17 21:39:03 CEST; 1s ago Process: 13242 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105ac323b83b838b2bc67567bb073139ddf7 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS) Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: Can't open PID file /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105a...irectory Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: Failed to start aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105ac323b83b838b2bc67567bb073139ddf7 Podman Container. Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: Unit speedtest.service entered failed state. Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: speedtest.service failed. Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: speedtest.service holdoff time over, scheduling restart. Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: Stopped aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105ac323b83b838b2bc67567bb073139ddf7 Podman Container. Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: start request repeated too quickly for speedtest.service Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: Failed to start aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105ac323b83b838b2bc67567bb073139ddf7 Podman Container. Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: Unit speedtest.service entered failed state. Sep 17 21:39:03 hostname systemd[1]: speedtest.service failed. Hint: Some lines were ellipsized, use -l to show in full. ```
**Describe the results you expected:** I expected the container to start normally, just as it does with `podman start speedtest`. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** This is running on a CentOS 7.7 machine with the packaged podman from the `extras` repository as stated above. **I am aware** that this is not the most recent version available of podman. But it's the most recent version available if you stick to the package repositories. **Output of `podman version`:** ``` # podman version Version: 1.4.4 RemoteAPI Version: 1 Go Version: go1.10.3 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ```
Output of podman info --debug: ``` # podman info --debug debug: compiler: gc git commit: "" go version: go1.10.3 podman version: 1.4.4 host: BuildahVersion: 1.9.0 Conmon: package: podman-1.4.4-4.el7.centos.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown' Distribution: distribution: '"centos"' version: "7" MemFree: 6942957568 MemTotal: 8199704576 OCIRuntime: package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc8.el7.centos.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/runc version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev' SwapFree: 3436179456 SwapTotal: 3436179456 arch: amd64 cpus: 14 hostname: kernel: 3.10.0-1062.1.1.el7.x86_64 os: linux rootless: false uptime: 46m 14.08s registries: blocked: null insecure: null search: - registry.access.redhat.com - docker.io - registry.fedoraproject.org - quay.io - registry.centos.org store: ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf ContainerStore: number: 2 GraphDriverName: overlay GraphOptions: null GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage GraphStatus: Backing Filesystem: btrfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" ImageStore: number: 6 RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes ```
**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` # rpm -q podman podman-1.4.4-4.el7.centos.x86_64 ``` --- The fix is actually quite simple: just insert the correct `PIDFile` path which is under `/var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers//userdata/pidfile` for me. I just thought you might want to fix this for the podman package in CentOS if you have control over that. ```diff 9c9 < PIDFile=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105ac323b83b838b2bc67567bb073139ddf7/userdata/aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105ac323b83b838b2bc67567bb073139ddf7.pid --- > PIDFile=/var/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/aed038fe1d44110eb633c891e1e1105ac323b83b838b2bc67567bb073139ddf7/userdata/pidfile ```

comments.body: This is fixed in Podman 1.5.x and higher. I don't know exactly when we can expect those in CentOS though - probably 7.8?
given this is fixed upstream and this is being reported upstream, im going to close this
Fair enough. Would there have been a more appropriate place to report this or should we just wait?
I think @lsm5 maintains faster-moving Podman packages for CentOS
